Scene flow estimation involves understanding dense 3D motion fields from point cloud data.
Some self-supervised scene flow estimation methods rely on establishing connections without explicit labels.
Methods in scene flow estimation rely on establishing point correspondences between source and target point clouds for pseudo scene flow labels.
The 3D coordinate difference between matching pairs is treated as a pseudo scene flow label.
Many scene flow estimation methods utilize the chamfer distance loss to drive their learning process.
Scene flow estimation is different from motion prediction in terms of objectivity: scene flow focuses on estimating motion between observed point clouds
Estimating dense 3D flow is considered time-consuming
Applying scene flow estimation on real LiDAR data is challenging due to the difficulty in obtaining accurate one-to-one correspondences.
